info.name: API Monitoring Backend
info.description: Combined Postman collection for auth, organization, billing, and projects modules.

request.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns session tokens or an MFA challenge.
Complete the second factor step after a password login.
Start the password reset flow.
Reset a password with a one-time reset token.
Change the current user's password.
Public health check for the auth module.
Create a new user account.
Fetch the currently authenticated user's profile.
Update the authenticated user's profile.
Soft-delete the authenticated user's account.
Admin-only lookup for a specific user.
Admin-only list endpoint with optional filters.
Admin-only restore for a soft-deleted user.
Admin-only suspend action for a user account.
Start TOTP, SMS, or email MFA setup.
Confirm a newly created MFA device.
Create an MFA challenge for a logged-in user.
Verify a challenge code without needing the user session.
List all MFA devices for the authenticated user.
Remove an MFA device. When it is the last active device, the server may require an MFA code.
Mark one MFA device as the primary device.
Generate a fresh set of backup codes.
Public backup-code verification endpoint.
Disable MFA for the authenticated user.
List active sessions for the authenticated user.
Revoke a specific session, excluding the current one.
Revoke every session except the current one.
Refresh an access token using a refresh token cookie/body payload.
Log out the current session and clear the refresh token cookie.
List organization projects with pagination and filters.
Create a new project inside an organization.
Fetch a single project by id.
Update mutable project fields.
Archive a project.
Restore an archived project to active.
Pause a project without archiving it.
Resume a paused project.
Delete a project. Requires owner-level access.
List API keys for a project. Raw keys are never returned.
Create an API key. The full key is returned only once in this response.
Fetch API key metadata by id.
Rename an API key without rotating it.
Enable a previously disabled API key.
Disable an API key without deleting it.
Rotate an API key. The old key is invalidated and the new raw key is returned once.
Delete or revoke an API key.
